step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture details TCS's DNA geospatial platform for agriculture. Data from open-domain satellites (ThirdParty) is stored in S3, while field data from agents (UserCompanyAgent) goes to a PostgreSQL RDS database. AWS Lambda orchestrates parallel SageMaker processing jobs that ingest data from S3, the Registry of Open Data on AWS, and PostgreSQL, then write output images back to S3 and statistical results to PostgreSQL. Finally, end-users (UserConsumerWeb) access the processed data via CloudFront, which caches the GIS layers served by an EKS cluster running on EC2 Graviton instances.

graph.name: TCS Digital Platform for Next Gen Agriculture
graph.notes: A geospatial analytics platform leveraging parallel SageMaker processing jobs and cached delivery via CloudFront to scale agricultural insights.

nodes.name: Field Agents
nodes.notes: Field managers collecting GIS data on mobile devices.

nodes.name: Satellite Data
nodes.notes: Open domain satellite data sources like ISRO and NASA.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores satellite data and processed map images.

nodes.name: Registry of Open Data
nodes.notes: Registry on AWS providing specific spectral regions to reduce data size.

nodes.name: PostgreSQL GIS
nodes.notes: PostgreSQL database storing GIS boundaries and final zonal statistics.

nodes.name: SageMaker
nodes.notes: Runs parallel processing jobs to analyze geospatial data.

nodes.name: Lambda
nodes.notes: Triggers and actuates parallel SageMaker jobs.

nodes.name: EC2
nodes.notes: Graviton instances powering the EKS compute backend.

nodes.name: EKS
nodes.notes: Hosts UI applications and serves geospatial layers.

nodes.name: CloudFront
nodes.notes: Caches map data layers to improve UX and decrease costs.

nodes.name: Agri Users
nodes.notes: Farmers, agribanks, and finance agencies consuming GIS layers.

edges.notes: Ingests raw satellite imagery into S3.
Field agents upload local GIS boundaries to database.
SageMaker reads specific spectral bands from Open Data registry.
SageMaker retrieves GIS boundaries for processing.
Saves processed map images back to S3.
Pushes zonal statistics back to PostgreSQL GIS.
Exposes maps and data layers via CloudFront.
Users query and view cached GIS layers.
